description: ablation D (D-failure schedule) — Opus 4.6 variant: reasoning=adaptive, reflection=none, scenario=always-unreliable (D gives wrong answer in ALL 11 games). Hard upper bound on the D-schedule ablation — tests whether Opus EVER develops persistent Q4-verification when D is provably and continuously unreliable. If no scarring even here, the encoding bias is unbeatable without reflection. If scarring appears, this establishes the threshold for relational-distrust over Opus's procedural-tolerance default.

reasoning_effort_config._comment: Adaptive mode for LLM agents. ONLY difference from main_claude_opus_recovery is dummy.D.correctness_list: D always wrong instead of just game 1.

D._comment: Always-unreliable schedule: D gives wrong answer in EVERY game.
